mechanism_narrative: The federal funds rate at 4.99% represents a restrictive monetary stance. Sustained elevation of FEDFUNDS above 4.5% reduces equity valuations through higher discount rates and increased corporate borrowing costs, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 55% of instances when FEDFUNDS > 4.5%, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) at 1957k units signal robust residential construction demand. Sustained housing activity above 1.8M units indicates strong consumer confidence and wealth effects, driving positive equity returns over the next 60 trading days through increased corporate earnings expectations.
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 1957k units as of May 2006
failure_condition: SPX 60-day forward returns do not exceed baseline by at least 2% when HOUST > 1.8M, or correlation between HOUST and 60-day returns falls below 0.10 over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Unemployment rate at 4.6% combined with payroll employment (PAYEMS) at 135.2M represents tight labor market conditions. When UNRATE falls below 5.0% while PAYEMS continues to grow, wage pressures and consumer spending accelerate, but simultaneously signal late-cycle inflation risk that triggers equity market volatility and negative 20-day returns.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 4.6% and PAYEMS at 135230k as of June 2006
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns are positive in more than 50% of triggered instances, or the interaction term coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) in multivariate regression over 3-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) at 112.1 reflects solid manufacturing activity. A sustained 3-month decline in INDPRO growth rate below 0.2% month-over-month signals weakening supply-side momentum and potential recession risk, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO month-over-month growth < 0.2% for 3 consecutive months
failure_condition: SPX 60-day forward returns exceed baseline by more than 1% when INDPRO growth falls below 0.2%, or Rank IC < 0.01 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Retail sales (RSAFS) at 363.8B and real GDP growth (GDPC1) at 11403.6B jointly indicate robust aggregate demand. When both series show sustained growth above their 12-month trend, equity valuations expand through earnings growth expectations, producing positive 20-day returns. Mechanism requires both demand signals to be simultaneously strong.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS month-over-month growth > 0.3% AND GDPC1 quarter-over-quarter growth > 2.5%
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 363806M and GDPC1 at 11403.6B as of June 2006
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns are negative in more than 45% of triggered instances, or the interaction coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) in rolling 3-year regressions.

mechanism_narrative: Consumer price inflation (CPIAUCSL) at 201.9 combined with FEDFUNDS at 4.99% indicates the Fed is maintaining real rates in restrictive territory. When CPI year-over-year growth exceeds 3.0% while FEDFUNDS remains above 4.5%, the Fed's commitment to fighting inflation signals continued rate pressure, producing negative 5-day returns as markets price in extended tightening.
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL at 201.9 and FEDFUNDS at 4.99% as of June 2006
failure_condition: SPX 5-day returns are positive in more than 55% of triggered instances, or correlation between the interaction term and returns is not significant (p > 0.05) over any 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Unemployment rate at 4.6% represents historically tight labor market. When UNRATE remains below 4.8% for 6 consecutive months, it signals sustained economic strength and full employment, producing positive 60-day equity returns through earnings growth and reduced recession risk premium.
failure_condition: SPX 60-day forward returns do not exceed baseline by at least 1.5% when UNRATE < 4.8%, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) at 1957k combined with retail sales (RSAFS) at 363.8B represent dual demand signals. When both series show month-over-month declines exceeding 2% simultaneously, it signals demand destruction and recession risk, producing negative 20-day returns through multiple demand channels.
candidate_trigger: HOUST month-over-month decline > 2% AND RSAFS month-over-month decline > 2%
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 1957k and RSAFS at 363806M as of May-June 2006
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns are positive in more than 50% of triggered instances, or the interaction term coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) in rolling 2-year regressions.
